definition: A temperature-controlled supply chain that ensures products retain their quality and safety from origin to destination.
A digital record providing comprehensive information about a product's lifecycle, enhancing transparency and traceability.
A South African public entity responsible for ensuring the quality, safety, and cold chain integrity of perishable products destined for export.
A network of physical objects embedded with sensors, software, and other technologies for the purpose of connecting and exchanging data with other devices and systems over the internet.
A cryptographic hash function that generates a unique, fixed-size 256-bit hash value from any input data, used for verifying data integrity.
A refrigerated container used for intermodal freight transport of temperature-sensitive cargo.
A set of defined rules that enable different applications to communicate with each other.
